spec_match.rationale: The inspector still maps :playing, :paused, :seeking, :muted, and :volume-locked through CSS selectors on a real video element.

interactive.rationale: The broken inline MP4 was replaced with local CC0 WebM/MP4 fixtures, but chrome-devtools-mcp was unavailable for live play/pause/seek/mute verification.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page now matches its promise to provide a playable video control for inspecting media pseudo-class states.

edge_cases.rationale: The page includes WebM and MP4 fallbacks plus relevant capability probes, but real seeking and volume-lock state still need browser verification.

references.rationale: The page includes ChromeStatus, MDN, and Selectors Level 4 reference links.

design_system.rationale: The code sample was moved back to design-system variables and the page keeps the existing flat card layout.

openQuestions.title: Verify video pseudo-class transitions
openQuestions.detail: Use chrome-devtools-mcp in Chrome 150+ to play, pause, seek, mute, and unmute /v150/media-element-pseudo-classes/state-inspector/, then confirm the CSS-only indicator rows and console/network state.

summary: Static repair resolves the broken video source by using local CC0 MDN flower video fixtures. Browser verification is still open because chrome-devtools-mcp is not exposed in this session.
